title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence

thm:I.2.1.summary: $G\text{-}\mathcal{I}\mathrm{soc}$ is a $v$-stack on perfect $\overline{\mathbb{F}}_q$-algebras with stratification by $b \in B(E,G)$. The Kottwitz / Newton stratification at the level of $G$-isocrystals.

thm:I.2.6.summary: Geometric points of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$: for $S = \mathrm{Spa}(C)$, the natural map $B(G) \to \mathrm{Bun}_G(S)/\cong$ is a bijection; vector bundles on $X_S$ decompose as $\bigoplus \mathcal{O}_{X_S}(\lambda)$. Identifies points of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ with $G$-isocrystals.

thm:I.2.11.summary: Pullback equivalence: $D(\mathrm{Bun}_G \times \mathrm{Div}^1, \overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell) \cong D(\mathrm{Bun}_G \times [*/W_E], \overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell) \cong D(\mathrm{Bun}_G, \overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell)$. Smith-style descent identifying derived categories on $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ with $W_E$-equivariant ones.

thm:I.3.2.summary: Fargues–Fontaine classification: vector bundles on the FF curve $X_C$ are direct sums of stable bundles $\mathcal{O}(\lambda)$ of slopes $\lambda \in \mathbb{Q}$, with isomorphism classes parameterized by isocrystals $\mathrm{Isoc}_E$. Imported foundational theorem.

thm:I.3.3.summary: Ample-bundle / Proj computation: for affinoid $S$ and vector bundle $\mathcal{E}$ on $X_S$, twists $\mathcal{E}(n)$ are globally generated for $n \gg 0$, and the graded ring $P = \bigoplus H^0(X_S, \mathcal{O}(n))$ recovers $X_S$ as $\mathrm{Proj}(P)$. Geometric input behind the relative FF curve.

thm:I.4.1.summary: $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ structure: $v$-stack, points $\leftrightarrow B(G)$, the Newton stratification with closed substacks $\mathrm{Bun}_G^{\le b}$, smoothness on each stratum. The geometric heart of the geometrization.

thm:I.4.2.summary: Moduli of subspaces: for $Z \to X_S$ embeddable as Zariski-closed in adic $\mathbb{P}^n_{X_S}$, $\mathcal{M}_Z \to S$ is representable in locally spatial diamonds, compactifiable, finite $\dim$. Geometric input for relative-curve constructions.

thm:I.5.1.summary: Semiorthogonal decomposition of $D(\mathrm{Bun}_G, \Lambda)$ along $b \in B(G)$ via excision; explicit description of each $D(\mathrm{Bun}_G^b, \Lambda)$ via pullback. The categorical analog of the Newton stratification.

thm:I.6.3.summary: Construction of the Langlands dual group $\widehat{G}_\Lambda$ via geometric Satake on the FF curve: a canonical isomorphism with $W_E$-action recovering the dual group. The Tannakian heart of the geometrization.

thm:I.7.2.summary: Hecke functors $T_V: D(\mathrm{Bun}_G) \to D(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ preserve compactness and universal local acyclicity, commute with BZ and Verdier duality. The geometric Hecke side of the local Langlands correspondence.

thm:I.8.1.summary: Moduli of $L$-parameters: a scheme $Z^1(W_E, \widehat{G})/\mathbb{Z}_\ell$ representing condensed $1$-cocycles $\varphi: W_E \to \widehat{G}(\Lambda)$. The space of $\widehat{G}$-valued $L$-parameters as an algebraic geometry object.

thm:I.8.2.summary: Cohomological vanishing on the parameter space: when $\ell \nmid |\pi_0 Z(G)|$, $H^i(\widehat{G}, \mathcal{O}(Z^1(W_E/P, \widehat{G}))) = 0$ for $i>0$ and invariants commute with base change. Lets the parameter space behave well derived-categorically.

thm:I.9.6.summary: Compatibility of the Fargues–Scholze correspondence: for $G = T$ a torus, $\pi \mapsto \varphi_\pi$ is the usual local Langlands; compatible with twisting, central characters, parabolic induction, and other classical reductions. The verification suite confirming the geometrization recovers known cases.

thm:I.10.1.summary: Excursion-operator construction: when $\ell \nmid |\pi_0 Z(G)|$, an exact $\mathrm{Rep}_\Lambda(Q^I)$-linear monoidal functor on a $\Lambda$-linear stable $\infty$-category $\mathcal{C}$ yields a compatible system parameterized by $L$-parameters. The categorical-Langlands-correspondence statement.

thm:II.0.1.summary: Local description of $\mathcal{Y}_C$: locally adic spectra $\mathrm{Spa}(B, B^+)$ with $B$ a principal-ideal-domain Banach algebra. Imported FF-curve foundations.

thm:II.0.3.summary: Body version of FF classification: vector bundles on $X_C$ classified by $\mathrm{Isoc}_E$. Body proof of thm:I.3.2.

thm:II.0.5.summary: Harder–Narasimhan polygon semicontinuity: for $S \in \mathrm{Perf}$, the function $s \mapsto \mathrm{HN}\text{-polygon}(\mathcal{E}|_{X_s})$ is semicontinuous in $S$. The variation-of-slopes theorem on the relative FF curve.

thm:III.0.2.summary: Body theorem for $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ structure: assembles $v$-stack property, point-set bijection with $B(G)$, Newton stratification, smoothness — the body proof of thm:I.4.1.

edges.note: Geometric points of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ identified via the FF classification of vector bundles.
Intro statement is the body theorem.
Point-set bijection $|\mathrm{Bun}_G| \leftrightarrow B(G)$ runs through the geometric-point identification.
Newton stratification uses HN-polygon semicontinuity.
Semiorthogonal decomposition runs along the Newton stratification.
Stratification of $G$-isocrystal stack provides the index set for the decomposition.
Geometric Satake construction runs over $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ stratification.
Hecke functors are built from the dual-group Tannakian description.
Compactness / ULA preservation runs through the strata.
Cohomology vanishing is on the parameter scheme.
Excursion operators are built from Hecke functors and dualities.
Excursion correspondence requires the parameter scheme as target.
Cohomological vanishing makes the categorical statement well-behaved.
Compatibility checks run on the categorical correspondence.
Intro statement = body theorem II.0.3.
Local PID description of $\mathcal{Y}_C$ underlies the classification proof.
Ample-twist / $\mathrm{Proj}$ result uses the FF classification.
Moduli of subspaces uses the relative-$\mathbb{P}^n$ / Proj construction.
Semicontinuity of HN polygon uses the FF classification on each fibre.
Smith descent runs over the $v$-stack structure of $\mathrm{Bun}_G$.

main_results.description: Section I. Statement of geometrization: $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ as a $v$-stack with Newton stratification; Hecke / dual-group / parameter scheme; categorical correspondence; classical compatibilities.

fargues_fontaine_curve.description: Sections II–III. The FF curve as a geometric object: vector-bundle classification, ampleness / $\mathrm{Proj}$ description, HN-polygon semicontinuity in families.

geometric_input.description: Sections I.2.11, I.4.2. Smith descent for $\mathrm{Bun}_G \times \mathrm{Div}^1$ and moduli of Zariski-closed subspaces.

Bun_G_geometry.description: Section III. Body theorem for $\mathrm{Bun}_G$ structure.

Scholze:Diamonds.title: Étale cohomology of diamonds
Scholze:Diamonds.role: Diamond / six-functor formalism heavily used throughout.

FarguesFontaine:Curve.title: Courbes et fibrés vectoriels en théorie de Hodge p-adique

Kottwitz.title: Isocrystals with additional structure
Kottwitz.role: Defines $B(G)$, the index set for the Newton stratification.

Borel79.title: Automorphic L-functions
Borel79.role: Classical Langlands compatibility statements at thm:I.9.6.

VLafforgue.title: Chtoucas pour les groupes réductifs et paramétrisation de Langlands
VLafforgue.role: Excursion-operator framework adapted to the local case at thm:I.10.1.

Scholze:Perfectoid.title: Perfectoid spaces
Scholze:Perfectoid.role: Foundational; tilting and perfectoid covers used throughout.
